sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Info action to introduce basic ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance into public discussion and outline guardrails for strategic direction.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Public discussion of treasury governance reform ideas, Basic propositions for treasury governance reform, Proposed treasury governance process sequence, Proposed thematic budget structure

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Current voting on treasury withdrawals has produced perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury governance should provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by both the governance system and the constitution.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The NCL should not be treated as the overall budget because it is only a vague initial approximation and is insufficient alongside isolated treasury withdrawals.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): DReps should vote on dedicated balanced budgets rather than individual project funding because direct comparison of individual proposals is cognitively inefficient and domain expertise is limited.
missing: Independent evidence for: Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: Current voting on treasury withdrawals has produced perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Treasury governance should provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
